message:
test(fcp-conformance/host-doctor-enums): pin OverallStatus + FreshnessLevel + CheckStatus + CheckSeverity wire format with hetero-rename rules (resolves flywheel_connectors-60uwh)

19 tests pinning fcp-host doctor's 4 enums — operator self-check
JSON output every dashboard, alerting rule, and CLI consumer reads.

CRITICAL: doctor module uses HETEROGENEOUS serde rename rules:
- OverallStatus → UPPERCASE (OK / WARN / FAIL)
- CheckStatus → UPPERCASE (OK / WARN / FAIL)
- CheckSeverity → lowercase (info / warning / critical)
- FreshnessLevel → snake_case (fresh / stale / too_stale / missing)

Drift to homogenise (e.g., flipping OverallStatus to lowercase to
'match the rest') would silently break every external consumer.

- 4 enums × full variant matrix with exact wire string
- Each rejects every wrong-case + unknown + empty input
- FreshnessLevel::default = Fresh (fail-safe default)
- Each enum implements Copy + variants distinct on PartialEq
- Cross-enum hetero-rename invariant test pins the 4 different
  case rules side-by-side
- 'ok' vs 'OK' case-collision: OverallStatus 'OK' is NOT a valid
  CheckSeverity (which has no Ok variant) — pinning the boundary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
